author: Corpus Analyst Rowan-C

dimensionSummaries.dimension.player-fantasy: The player acts as both field adventurer and responsible engineer, restoring a damaged rail network while coordinating closely with an embodied companion.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.core-loop: The cadence moves from tower guidance to route restoration, train travel, settlement or dungeon action, a returned map segment, and a larger rail network of destinations and services.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.verbs: Stylus movement, attacks, tool gestures, route drawing, train speed and whistle control, microphone flute play, and directed companion paths form a hardware-shaped verb set.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.world-state: Restored tracks persistently add reachable branches, tower progress changes regional access, and acquired tools or Phantom forms alter what coordinated actions are possible.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.topology: Settlements and challenge spaces are connected by constrained rail graphs whose junctions, hazards, gates, and alternate lines make route planning different from free overland motion.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.gating-revisit: New track segments, tools, songs, and companion capabilities open destinations and allow earlier routes or tower floors to be approached with broader options.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.puzzle-information: Puzzles externalize coordination through drawn companion paths, synchronized positions, item interactions, rail diagrams, audible melodies, and visible timing relationships.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.dungeon-structure: Regional temples teach item rules, while the central tower is revisited in newly available sections where Link and Phantom Zelda solve increasingly interdependent room chains.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.exploration-reward: New rail branches expose stations, errands, rabbits, treasure, optional challenge spaces, and faster connections that give network expansion value beyond the next mandatory stop.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.growth-economy: Health, tools, treasure, train parts, cargo and passenger rewards, and collectible upgrades support both personal progression and investment in the travel layer.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.combat-boss: Touch combat is joined by train cannon encounters and companion-assisted fights, while bosses synthesize item use, positioning, timing, and readable vulnerable phases.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.npc-quest: Passengers, townspeople, guardians, merchants, and Zelda provide requests and reactions; careful transport and restored access visibly connect service work to the world graph.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.narrative-performance: Zelda remains an active companion whose movement, fear, humor, possession of Phantoms, and joint puzzle actions perform the partnership during play rather than only in cutscenes.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.camera-blocking: The overhead field camera shows both partners and puzzle dependencies, while train framing and the upper display expose forward hazards and route context at travel scale.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.teaching-feedback: Drawn routes, partner movement, track restoration, whistle responses, passenger reactions, tool animations, and puzzle-completion cues make action consequences immediately visible or audible.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.ui-input: The touch screen handles avatar and route commands, the map holds planned rail lines, and the microphone becomes an explicit performance input for the Spirit Flute.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.recovery-accessibility: Failed coordination can be redrawn, route plans can be changed before danger, checkpoints bound repeated work, and optional preparation can reduce combat or travel pressure.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.perspective-implementation: A 2.5D overhead view supports precise drawn paths and partner readability, whereas forward train views make a one-dimensional rail graph feel spatial and hazardous.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.hardware-multiplayer: The DS dual screens, stylus, microphone, local wireless, and download play shape both the single-player adventure and a separate two-to-four-player competitive mode.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.production-runtime: The design depends on spline-like rail navigation, live route editing, companion pathfinding, possession states, microphone pitch input, passenger evaluation, and persistent network unlocks.

observationThemes.context: After resolving a regional objective, a missing portion of the Spirit Tracks is restored on the world map.
preconditions: A destination or branch is absent from the usable rail graph
The linked tower or regional objective has been completed
playerInput: Receive and inspect the restored map segment
Draw a train route over the newly available track
before: Stations beyond the missing track cannot be reached
The train's route choices are limited to the current graph
action: Apply the restored track state and select a route through it
after: New rail edges and destinations become traversable
Earlier junctions gain additional route choices
feedback: The train can enter the restored branch and arrive at a new location
consequences: Existing network space is reinterpreted through new connections and revisits
observationThemes.factualDescription: Completing progression objectives restores missing rail segments on the map, persistently adding routes that let the train reach new stations and branches.
observationThemes.interpretation: Representing progression as an added graph edge makes world change concrete and lets a linear vehicle support meaningful route growth and return planning.

observationThemes.context: Before and during a train journey, the player chooses a path that may include passengers, hazards, optional stations, or collectible opportunities.
preconditions: The train and at least one connected route are available
A destination, passenger, cargo, or optional lead has been selected
playerInput: Control speed and whistle in response to signs, traffic, and threats
before: Rewards and optional destinations on the route remain unclaimed
action: Operate the train while honoring route and service conditions
after: The request or detour can resolve and issue its reward
feedback: The route line and approaching junctions remain visible
Passengers, signs, enemies, and arrival scoring respond to driving behavior
consequences: Treasure or payment supports equipment and train growth
Optional errands make restored branches socially and economically meaningful
observationThemes.factualDescription: The player draws train routes and adjusts speed or whistle during travel; passenger and delivery tasks evaluate the trip and can grant rewards or expose optional content.
observationThemes.interpretation: Giving travel operational duties turns a connector between levels into a service fantasy where route knowledge, care, and economy reinforce one another.

observationThemes.context: In the tower, Zelda can inhabit a Phantom and receive a drawn route while Link remains independently controlled.
preconditions: A compatible Phantom has been made vulnerable
Zelda is able to possess it
The room requires two positions or complementary capabilities
playerInput: Enable Zelda to enter the Phantom
Draw a route for the Phantom
Move Link and time the partners' actions
before: The obstacle cannot be resolved by Link alone
The hostile Phantom is not yet an allied body
action: Convert the enemy body into a directed companion and coordinate both actors
after: The Phantom follows the route and performs its form-specific role
A joint path, switch, defense, or transfer becomes possible
feedback: Possession visibly changes the Phantom's allegiance
The drawn line, partner movement, and room response display the coordination chain
consequences: Later Phantom types expand the partnership's puzzle vocabulary and embodied relationship
observationThemes.factualDescription: Zelda can possess vulnerable Phantoms; the player draws her route and coordinates that controlled body with Link to solve room dependencies neither can complete alone.
observationThemes.interpretation: Turning a companion's narrative presence into a separately routed body lets relationship development and puzzle complexity share the same observable action system.

observationThemes.context: A route, partner command, or Spirit Flute phrase asks the player to coordinate touch or breath input with information distributed across the DS displays.
preconditions: The relevant tool or travel mode is active
The game has presented a visible route, partner target, or melody cue
playerInput: Draw or touch the requested spatial command
For flute play, move the stylus and blow into the microphone in the shown sequence
before: The route, partner action, or musical event is unresolved
Reference information is visible but has not been performed
action: Translate the displayed information into the hardware-specific input
after: The avatar, partner, train, or musical state executes the command
The linked obstacle or event can change
feedback: Pointer paths and overhead blocking show spatial execution
Audible notes and display changes confirm microphone and touch recognition
consequences: The same device supports both solitary coordination and a separate local competitive mode
observationThemes.factualDescription: The original separates active overhead action and reference information across two displays, uses stylus paths for spatial commands, and uses touch plus microphone breath for flute performance.
observationThemes.interpretation: When hardware input closely resembles the represented act, interface feedback can teach an unusual verb through imitation instead of a long command explanation.

observationThemes.context: During rail travel, hostile trains or enemies threaten the vehicle while their direction and the available junctions remain visible on the route map.
preconditions: The train is moving on a route with a live hazard
At least one speed, junction, alternate-line, whistle, or cannon response is available
playerInput: Revise the route or adjust speed
Use the cannon or whistle when appropriate
before: The current trajectory risks collision or damage
The journey has not yet failed
action: Respond before the hazard reaches the same rail segment
after: The train avoids, passes, or defeats the threat
The journey continues toward its destination
feedback: The map shows moving danger and junction structure
Vehicle speed, impacts, enemy response, and route motion confirm the result
consequences: Planning can recover a dangerous trip without restarting immediately
Boss and vehicle encounters reuse the same readable timing-and-response foundation
observationThemes.factualDescription: Rail hazards expose their movement on the route display, and the player can alter course, speed, or weapon use to avoid collision and continue the journey.
observationThemes.interpretation: A constrained network remains tactically expressive when threat intent is legible and the player can revise a plan before failure, but it requires reliable path simulation and junction state.

contributionHypotheses: Restoring edges to a visible travel graph can make world repair, access growth, and revisit value the same state change.
A companion becomes mechanically present when the player can route a distinct body whose capabilities complement the protagonist.
Constrained vehicle travel can remain interactive when route revision, service conditions, and readable hazards create decisions throughout the trip.
uniqueOrNonportable: The Spirit Tracks cosmology, named tower and flute, train and Phantom designs, character identities, exact rail map, melodies, dialogue, and story events are nonportable.
Specific partner routes, dungeon layouts, boss answers, passenger scripts, and microphone melodies remain source evidence rather than reusable content.
openQuestions: A later service-state review should document how the retired DS online functions affect reproducibility while keeping local and single-player observations distinct.
